what: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis
title: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis
text: Climate Departure Analysis ERA5-Land Reanalysis

title: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis
text: cd computes climate anomalies ERA5-Land reanalysis data custom areas interest. downloads raw climate data, derives variables (VPD, RH, soil moisture), publishes Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs STAC catalog, provides consumer functions extract zonal statistics, compute baselines anomalies arbitrary reference periods, run trend analysis.

title: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis
Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis
code: library(cd)  # Load a STAC catalog and an area of interest catalog <- cd_catalog(   system.file("extdata", "example_catalog.json", package = "cd") ) aoi <- sf::st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE )  # Extract zonal mean time series ts <- cd_extract(catalog, aoi)  # Compute baseline and anomalies bl <- cd_baseline(ts, baseline_years = 1951:1955) ano <- cd_anomaly(ts, bl)  # Trend analysis trn <- cd_trend(ano, trend_start = 1951)  # Reporting table cd_summary(trn)  # Compare time windows directly cd_compare(ts, window_a = 1956:1960, window_b = 1951:1955)

title: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis
text: Function reference Vignette (coming soon)

what: cd: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis — cd-package
title: cd: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis — cd-package
text: Compute climate anomalies ERA5-Land reanalysis data custom areas interest. Producer functions download ERA5-Land monthly means, derive variables (VPD, RH, soil moisture), publish Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs static STAC catalog. Consumer functions query catalog, crop AOI, compute baselines anomalies arbitrary reference periods, run trend analysis (Mann-Kendall Theil-Sen).

title: cd: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis — cd-package
text: Maintainer: Allan Irvine al@newgraphenvironment.com (ORCID) contributors: New Graph Environment Ltd. [copyright holder]

title: Compute climate anomalies — cd_anomaly
text: Calculates departure baseline year. Uses cd_variables() determine anomaly type: absolute deviation temperature, VPD, RH; percent normal precipitation soil moisture.

title: Compute climate anomalies — cd_anomaly
text: x tibble cd_extract() columns variable, period, year, value. baseline tibble cd_baseline() columns variable, period, baseline_mean. cap_pct Numeric. Cap percent--normal anomalies. Values beyond +/- cap_pct clamped. Default 200.

title: Compute climate anomalies — cd_anomaly
text: tibble columns variable, period, year, anomaly, anomaly_type, unit.

title: Compute climate anomalies — cd_anomaly
code: catalog <- cd_catalog(   system.file("extdata", "example_catalog.json", package = "cd") ) aoi <- sf::st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE ) ts <- cd_extract(catalog, aoi)  # Compute anomalies relative to early-period baseline # Absolute deviation for temperature; percent of normal for precipitation bl <- cd_baseline(ts, baseline_years = 1951:1955) cd_anomaly(ts, bl) #> # A tibble: 10 × 6 #>    variable period  year anomaly anomaly_type unit  #>    <chr>    <chr>  <int>   <dbl> <chr>        <chr> #>  1 tmean    annual  1951 -1.43   absolute     °C    #>  2 tmean    annual  1952 -0.571  absolute     °C    #>  3 tmean    annual  1953  0.562  absolute     °C    #>  4 tmean    annual  1954  1.22   absolute     °C    #>  5 tmean    annual  1955  0.218  absolute     °C    #>  6 tmean    annual  1956 -1.43   absolute     °C    #>  7 tmean    annual  1957  0.0707 absolute     °C    #>  8 tmean    annual  1958  0.106  absolute     °C    #>  9 tmean    annual  1959  1.98   absolute     °C    #> 10 tmean    annual  1960 -0.171  absolute     °C

title: Compute climatological baseline — cd_baseline
text: Computes mean value reference period variable period combination. result used cd_anomaly() calculate departures.

title: Compute climatological baseline — cd_baseline
text: x tibble cd_extract() columns variable, period, year, value. baseline_years Integer vector years average. Default 1981:2010 (WMO standard).

title: Compute climatological baseline — cd_baseline
text: tibble columns variable, period, baseline_mean.

title: Compute climatological baseline — cd_baseline
code: catalog <- cd_catalog(   system.file("extdata", "example_catalog.json", package = "cd") ) aoi <- sf::st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE ) ts <- cd_extract(catalog, aoi)  # Early period baseline — pre-warming reference for departure communication cd_baseline(ts, baseline_years = 1951:1955) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   variable period baseline_mean #>   <chr>    <chr>          <dbl> #> 1 tmean    annual         -1.44  # Later period baseline — shows how baseline choice affects anomaly magnitude cd_baseline(ts, baseline_years = 1956:1960) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   variable period baseline_mean #>   <chr>    <chr>          <dbl> #> 1 tmean    annual         -1.33

title: Clear the cd cache — cd_cache_clear
text: Removes cached files cd cache directory.

title: Clear the cd cache — cd_cache_clear
text: cache_dir Character. Override default cache location.

title: Clear the cd cache — cd_cache_clear
text: Invisibly returns number files removed.

title: Show cd cache info — cd_cache_info
text: Reports cache location size.

title: Show cd cache info — cd_cache_info
text: cache_dir Character. Override default cache location.

title: Show cd cache info — cd_cache_info
text: list path, n_files, size_mb.

what: Get cd cache directory path — cd_cache_path
title: Get cd cache directory path — cd_cache_path
text: Returns path cd cache directory. Creates exist.

title: Get cd cache directory path — cd_cache_path
Get cd cache directory path — cd_cache_path
text: cache_dir Character. Override default cache location. NULL, uses rappdirs::user_cache_dir("cd").

title: Get cd cache directory path — cd_cache_path
text: Character path cache directory.

title: Get cd cache directory path — cd_cache_path

what: Load and parse a STAC catalog — cd_catalog
title: Load and parse a STAC catalog — cd_catalog
text: Reads static STAC catalog JSON file returns tidy tibble available climate data COGs. entry point consumer-side workflows.

title: Load and parse a STAC catalog — cd_catalog
Load and parse a STAC catalog — cd_catalog
text: catalog Path URL STAC catalog JSON file. Defaults cd_catalog_default().

title: Load and parse a STAC catalog — cd_catalog
text: tibble columns: variable Climate variable short name (e.g., "tmean"). period Temporal aggregation period (e.g., "annual"). href Resolved path URL COG file.

title: Load and parse a STAC catalog — cd_catalog
code: cd_catalog(   system.file("extdata", "example_catalog.json", package = "cd") ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   variable period href                                                           #>   <chr>    <chr>  <chr>                                                          #> 1 tmean    annual /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/cd/extdata/./example_climate.…

title: Default STAC catalog URL — cd_catalog_default
text: Returns default S3-hosted STAC catalog URL cd package. Override options(cd.catalog_url = "...").

title: Default STAC catalog URL — cd_catalog_default
text: Character URL.

what: Write a SpatRaster as a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF — cd_cog_write
title: Write a SpatRaster as a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF — cd_cog_write
text: Wrapper around terra::writeRaster() COG format defaults. Compression GDAL creation options parameterized flexibility across data types use cases.

title: Write a SpatRaster as a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF — cd_cog_write
code: cd_cog_write(x, path, overwrite = FALSE, gdal = c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE"), ...)

title: Write a SpatRaster as a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF — cd_cog_write
text: x terra::SpatRaster write. path Character. Output file path. overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing file. Default FALSE. gdal Character vector GDAL creation options. Default c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE"). common options: "COMPRESS=LZW", "COMPRESS=ZSTD", "OVERVIEW_RESAMPLING=AVERAGE", "BLOCKSIZE=512". ... Additional arguments passed terra::writeRaster().

title: Write a SpatRaster as a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF — cd_cog_write
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Write a SpatRaster as a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF — cd_cog_write
code: r <- terra::rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = rnorm(100)) tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".tif") cd_cog_write(r, tmp, overwrite = TRUE)  # Custom compression cd_cog_write(r, tmp, overwrite = TRUE, gdal = c("COMPRESS=LZW"))

title: Compare arbitrary time windows — cd_compare
text: Computes mean value two user-defined time windows difference . Enables custom comparisons beyond standard baseline anomalies.

title: Compare arbitrary time windows — cd_compare
text: x tibble cd_extract() columns variable, period, year, value. window_a Integer vector years first window. window_b Integer vector years second window. method Character. Comparison method: "mean_diff" mean_a - mean_b, "pct_change" percentage change relative window_b.

title: Compare arbitrary time windows — cd_compare
text: tibble columns variable, period, mean_a, mean_b, difference, method.

title: Compare arbitrary time windows — cd_compare
code: catalog <- cd_catalog(   system.file("extdata", "example_catalog.json", package = "cd") ) aoi <- sf::st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE ) ts <- cd_extract(catalog, aoi)  # How has the recent period shifted from the early period? cd_compare(ts, window_a = 1956:1960, window_b = 1951:1955) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   variable period mean_a mean_b difference method    #>   <chr>    <chr>   <dbl>  <dbl>      <dbl> <chr>     #> 1 tmean    annual  -1.33  -1.44      0.110 mean_diff  # Same comparison as percentage change cd_compare(ts, window_a = 1956:1960, window_b = 1951:1955, method = "pct_change") #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   variable period mean_a mean_b difference method     #>   <chr>    <chr>   <dbl>  <dbl>      <dbl> <chr>      #> 1 tmean    annual  -1.33  -1.44       7.63 pct_change

what: Crop and mask a raster to an AOI — cd_crop
title: Crop and mask a raster to an AOI — cd_crop
text: Reads COG (local remote) crops user-supplied area interest polygon. Works local file paths remote URLs via GDAL's /vsicurl/.

title: Crop and mask a raster to an AOI — cd_crop
Crop and mask a raster to an AOI — cd_crop
text: href Character. Path URL COG raster file. aoi sf SpatVector polygon crop .

title: Crop and mask a raster to an AOI — cd_crop
text: terra::SpatRaster cropped masked AOI.

title: Crop and mask a raster to an AOI — cd_crop
code: href <- system.file("extdata", "example_climate.tif", package = "cd") aoi <- sf::st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE ) r <- cd_crop(href, aoi) r #> class       : SpatRaster  #> size        : 4, 5, 10  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 0.25, 0.25  (x, y) #> extent      : -126.875, -125.625, 53.875, 54.875  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (EPSG:4326)  #> source(s)   : memory #> varname     : example_climate  #> names       :      1951,      1952,       1953,        1954,      1955,      1956, ...  #> min values  : -3.026460, -2.066120, -1.0476445, -0.33427483, -1.447783, -2.927964, ...  #> max values  : -2.723417, -1.877674, -0.7369902, -0.09393072, -1.106457, -2.837888, ...

what: Derive climate variables from raw ERA5-Land fields — cd_derive
title: Derive climate variables from raw ERA5-Land fields — cd_derive
text: Computes VPD, RH, composite soil moisture, monthly tmax/tmin raw ERA5-Land GRIB files downloaded cd_fetch(). Also converts temperature Kelvin Celsius precipitation m/day mm/month.

title: Derive climate variables from raw ERA5-Land fields — cd_derive
code: cd_derive(   input_dir,   output_dir,   variables = c("vpd", "rh", "soil_moisture"),   force = FALSE )

title: Derive climate variables from raw ERA5-Land fields — cd_derive
text: input_dir Character path containing raw GRIB files cd_fetch(). output_dir Character path write derived rasters. variables Character vector variables derive. Default derives : VPD, RH, soil moisture, tmax, tmin. force Logical. Re-derive even output files exist. Default FALSE.

title: Derive climate variables from raw ERA5-Land fields — cd_derive
text: Character vector derived file paths.

title: Derive climate variables from raw ERA5-Land fields — cd_derive

what: Extract zonal mean time series for an AOI — cd_extract
title: Extract zonal mean time series for an AOI — cd_extract
text: variable period catalog, crops COG AOI computes spatial mean per year (band). Returns tidy tibble raw climate values suitable cd_baseline(), cd_anomaly(), cd_trend().

title: Extract zonal mean time series for an AOI — cd_extract
code: cd_extract(   catalog,   aoi,   variables = catalog$variable,   periods = catalog$period,   years = NULL )

title: Extract zonal mean time series for an AOI — cd_extract
text: catalog tibble cd_catalog() columns variable, period, href. aoi sf SpatVector polygon. variables Character vector variables extract. Defaults variables catalog. periods Character vector periods extract. Defaults periods catalog. years Optional integer vector filter specific years.

title: Extract zonal mean time series for an AOI — cd_extract
text: tibble columns: variable Climate variable short name. period Temporal aggregation period. year Year (integer). value Spatial mean climate value AOI.

title: Extract zonal mean time series for an AOI — cd_extract
code: catalog <- cd_catalog(   system.file("extdata", "example_catalog.json", package = "cd") ) aoi <- sf::st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE ) cd_extract(catalog, aoi) #> # A tibble: 10 × 4 #>    variable period  year  value #>    <chr>    <chr>  <int>  <dbl> #>  1 tmean    annual  1951 -2.87  #>  2 tmean    annual  1952 -2.01  #>  3 tmean    annual  1953 -0.881 #>  4 tmean    annual  1954 -0.223 #>  5 tmean    annual  1955 -1.22  #>  6 tmean    annual  1956 -2.88  #>  7 tmean    annual  1957 -1.37  #>  8 tmean    annual  1958 -1.34  #>  9 tmean    annual  1959  0.537 #> 10 tmean    annual  1960 -1.61

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch
text: Downloads ERA5-Land data Copernicus Climate Data Store (CDS) specified bounding box, time period, set variables. Uses two CDS products: monthly means variables, daily statistics tmax/tmin (available monthly means).

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch
code: cd_fetch(   years,   months = 1:12,   variables = cd_variables()$variable,   bbox = c(60, -140, 48, -114),   output_dir,   source = "era5_land",   force = FALSE )

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch
text: years Integer vector years download. months Integer vector months (1-12). Default 1:12. variables Character vector cd variable names fetch raw inputs . Default fetches inputs needed 7 variables. bbox Numeric vector c(north, west, south, east) degrees. Default covers British Columbia. output_dir Character path write downloaded files. source Character. Data source identifier. Currently "era5_land" supported. force Logical. Re-download even files exist. Default FALSE.

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch
text: Character vector downloaded file paths (GRIBs extracted zip archives).

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download January 2024 for BC cd_fetch(years = 2024, months = 1, output_dir = "data/raw")  # Download full year for custom bbox cd_fetch(years = 2023, bbox = c(55, -128, 53, -124), output_dir = "data/raw") } # }

title: Valid temporal aggregation periods — cd_periods
text: Returns set temporal periods supported cd package. Used input validation iteration extraction analysis functions.

title: Valid temporal aggregation periods — cd_periods
text: include_monthly Logical. TRUE, appends 12 calendar months (Jan, Feb, ..., Dec) seasonal periods. Default FALSE.

title: Valid temporal aggregation periods — cd_periods
text: Character vector period names.

what: Format trend results as a reporting table — cd_summary
title: Format trend results as a reporting table — cd_summary
text: Joins trend statistics variable metadata cd_variables() computes Total Change (slope x years). Returns tibble ready DT::datatable() gt::gt().

title: Format trend results as a reporting table — cd_summary
Format trend results as a reporting table — cd_summary
text: trend tibble cd_trend(). region_name Optional character label AOI. provided, adds Region column.

title: Format trend results as a reporting table — cd_summary
text: tibble columns Parameter, Period, Slope, Years, Total Change, Unit, p-value, optionally Region.

title: Format trend results as a reporting table — cd_summary
code: catalog <- cd_catalog(   system.file("extdata", "example_catalog.json", package = "cd") ) aoi <- sf::st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE ) ts <- cd_extract(catalog, aoi) trn <- cd_trend(ts, trend_start = 1951)  # Reporting table with Total Change = slope * years cd_summary(trn) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   Parameter        Period Slope Years `Total Change` Unit  `p-value` #>   <chr>            <chr>  <dbl> <int>          <dbl> <chr>     <dbl> #> 1 Mean temperature Annual 0.128    10            1.3 °C        0.592  # Add region label for multi-AOI reports cd_summary(trn, region_name = "Example AOI") #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   Parameter        Period Slope Years `Total Change` Unit  `p-value` Region      #>   <chr>            <chr>  <dbl> <int>          <dbl> <chr>     <dbl> <chr>       #> 1 Mean temperature Annual 0.128    10            1.3 °C        0.592 Example AOI

title: Compute trend statistics — cd_trend
text: Runs Mann-Kendall significance test Theil-Sen slope estimator time series data variable, period, trend start year.

title: Compute trend statistics — cd_trend
text: x tibble cd_extract() cd_anomaly() columns variable, period, year, either value anomaly. trend_start Integer vector start years trend windows. Default c(1950, 1980).

title: Compute trend statistics — cd_trend
text: tibble columns variable, period, trend_start, slope, intercept, mk_pvalue, n_years.

title: Compute trend statistics — cd_trend
code: catalog <- cd_catalog(   system.file("extdata", "example_catalog.json", package = "cd") ) aoi <- sf::st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE ) ts <- cd_extract(catalog, aoi)  # Trend on raw values cd_trend(ts, trend_start = 1951) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   variable period trend_start slope intercept mk_pvalue n_years #>   <chr>    <chr>        <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <int> #> 1 tmean    annual        1951 0.128     -253.     0.592      10  # Also works on anomalies — uses 'anomaly' column automatically bl <- cd_baseline(ts, baseline_years = 1951:1955) ano <- cd_anomaly(ts, bl) cd_trend(ano, trend_start = 1951) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   variable period trend_start slope intercept mk_pvalue n_years #>   <chr>    <chr>        <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <int> #> 1 tmean    annual        1951 0.128     -251.     0.592      10

title: Climate variable metadata — cd_variables
text: Returns tibble metadata seven climate variables supported cd package. Used internally unit labels, anomaly type routing, ERA5-Land API variable names.

title: Climate variable metadata — cd_variables
text: tibble columns: variable Short name used throughout package. long_name Human-readable label plots tables. unit Measurement unit (degree C, percent, Pa). anomaly_type "absolute" direct departures, "pct_normal" percent--normal anomalies. era5_name ERA5-Land variable name CDS API requests, NA derived variables.

title: Climate variable metadata — cd_variables
code: cd_variables() #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>   variable      long_name               unit  anomaly_type era5_name           #>   <chr>         <chr>                   <chr> <chr>        <chr>               #> 1 tmean         Mean temperature        °C    absolute     2m_temperature      #> 2 tmax          Maximum temperature     °C    absolute     NA                  #> 3 tmin          Minimum temperature     °C    absolute     NA                  #> 4 prcp          Precipitation           %     pct_normal   total_precipitation #> 5 vpd           Vapour pressure deficit Pa    absolute     NA                  #> 6 rh            Relative humidity       %     absolute     NA                  #> 7 soil_moisture Soil moisture           %     pct_normal   NA                  cd_variables()$variable #> [1] "tmean"         "tmax"          "tmin"          "prcp"          #> [5] "vpd"           "rh"            "soil_moisture"

title: cd (development version)
text: Initial development version. Consumer producer pipelines ERA5-Land climate departure analysis.
